title: Unity-gain buffer before the ADC
prompt: My high-impedance sensor sags when the Arduino (ATmega328) ADC samples it. Add a unity-gain op-amp (LM358) buffer between the sensor and the ADC pin so the ADC sees a low-impedance copy of the sensor voltage. Firmware reads the buffered node. For the bench test the buffered output equals the sensor voltage, about 2.5 V.

abstraction.reason: the LM358 buffer has no simulatable SPICE subcircuit; the buffered OUTPUT (a low-impedance copy of the sensor voltage) is modelled with a #105 voltage_source stand-in
